dungeon.flavor: The first descent. Stone corridors and stitched-together rooms; everything kills in one or two hits if you stop paying attention.

dungeon_dark.flavor: The torches go out a level deep. Same stones, different nightmare — silhouettes and ambushes where the light should have been.

mines.flavor: Orcish picks struck this rock for a hundred years. Echoes of work-songs, mounds of scrap iron, and the orcs themselves still digging.

lair.flavor: Damp earth and gnawed bones. Beasts run in packs here; the air smells of fur and bog. No walls — only territory.

forest.flavor: Crooked oaks lean inward as if listening. Spriggans and faerie things flit between trunks; the bark is older than your bot.

swamp.flavor: Brackish water, drowned roots, things that aren't logs. Step wrong and the floor breathes. Hydras at the bottom.

snake.flavor: Coiled caverns of scoured stone. Naga twine in patrol-spirals; the floor is warm wherever they've recently passed.

shoals.flavor: Salt-bleached reef and tide pools at the edge of the world. Merfolk hunt in lines; the sea lifts the cavern floor.

orc.flavor: Beogh's stronghold. Banners, butcher tables, an altar slick with blessing-blood. The warband is here, and so is its warlord.

vaults.flavor: Geometric halls of polished stone, cut by guildsmiths. Rare loot, regimented patrols, and a Vault Sentinel that does not blink.

crypt.flavor: Catacombs lit by lichlight. The dead don't know they're dead. Wraiths drift through walls; mummies count their prayers.

tomb.flavor: A pyramid of pharaohs and their grudges. Every chamber a curse, every corridor older than the dungeon itself.

forge.flavor: Demonic workshops where weapons drink heat instead of metal. Lava channels, anvils that hum on their own, and the smith-king at the heart.

glacier.flavor: Blue ice in slabs the size of houses. Frostbite kills as fast as the ice giants do; the cold has weight.

slime.flavor: Acidic chambers where the floor pulses. Jellies split and re-form; the Royal Jelly at the bottom is not a metaphor.

hive.flavor: Honeycomb walls and the buzz of a thousand wings. Bees defend territory like an army; the queen is the size of a cart.

labyrinth.flavor: A maze that rearranges itself when you blink. The Minotaur is somewhere in here, and so is everyone who ever entered.

abyss.flavor: The walls drift. Geometry won't hold still long enough to map. Things from outside the dungeon hunt here; reality is the prey.

pandemonium.flavor: An infinite plane of demon-lord palaces. Each lord wants something different; none of them want you to leave.

zot.flavor: The deepest sanctum. Orbs of menacing power; the Orb of Zot itself, and the killers it's worth.

elf.flavor: Mirrored halls and cool-burning lanterns. Elven mages cast in unison; even the silence is choreographed.

spider.flavor: Webbing in the corners, cocoons in the alcoves, eyes everywhere. Spiders the size of dogs, spiders the size of horses.

temple.flavor: A neutral ground at the dungeon's surface — every god's altar in a single hall. Bless here; the gods watch the rest of the run.

depths.flavor: Deeper still than the Vaults — natural caverns warped by long pressure. The bridge between civilization and the bottom of the world.
